item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The image could not be sourced reliably, as it's an anachronistic image of an urban legend. As such, it is assumed not compliant with CC 3.0 and has been removed.

item.title: If you want to play up the RPG Maker connection...
item.content:encoded: If you want to play up the RPG Maker connection… Then change the company from Diabolism Incorporated into a reference to Enterbrain?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love it. I enjoy "faulty magical product" type scps and this one fits in perfectly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Liked it until the Cult-Maker bit, then it killed the piece for me. Leaps over silly and absurd and just makes me roll my eyes. No-vote.

EDIT: Came around to it. Right amount of absurd as well as amusing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I quite liked the original, and I loved this. Traitorous box indeed!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It seems to be a love-it-or-hate-it aspect of this rewrite, but worth noting that the Cult Maker thing didn't do it for me, especially the note; it seemed an odd piece of flippancy right at the end of the article. I also thought the prominence given to the newly-added computer hardware nature of the object makes the log seem a bit like it's the object accessing the database using the researcher's ID, which I gather isn't the intent. That said, the rewrite adds depth to the original, and the challenge-response nature of 'Does the black moon howl' is a lot clearer and better written here. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it right up until the "cult-maker" part.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The original didn't do anything for me years ago. This is better but it isn't enough to sway an upvote from me. The DJOK TOURAFF line though…I know I have seen it before. Maybe in a old creepypasta or article. It will probably come to me when I'm not thinking about it. Neutral.

Edit: It was from the original article. Brain fart on my part.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Loved the Cult Maker concept and generally the whole thing. Seeing the image confused me, as I remembered seeing it before, but now I understand and shall go upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel pretty put off by the 'cult maker' concept when spun along psuedo-Wondertainment lines (I know, not exactly like Wondertainment, but I found the trope a bit tiresome) but… I still like it enough to upvote, so take that FWIW.

EDIT: "©2012 Diabolism Incorporated" was gold, though

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah, that'd be excessive and also mean. I just wanted to make sure you had the whole picture, since ye said you were out of the loop.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
TRAITOROUS BOX

Okay, I laughed more than I should have.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was six months ago, with basically no activity since. Is this a request to take the page down?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: http://05command.wikidot.com/forum/t-623202#post-1706285

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why a "comatose state" and not just a coma?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Login sequence was awesome.

The main article was very meh. OK, it's a skull that sends you kerayzee. It can connect to computers and can reprogramme your mind to turn you into a Foundation Dreadlord. It got funding on Kickstarter. It seems less like a compelling, cohesive article, more a few injokes about nerd culture and gaming tossed together into an SCP. And it's not laugh-out-loud funny enough for me to see it as a viable J-list article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bleh. Was never a fan of the original, due to it having no content, but this feels like more on an extension of that lack of content. The kickstarter bit and and the computery mumbo-jumbo don't add anything for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So… why was this Keter? There's no description of what this thing does/did other than the implications in the (admittedly humorous) addendum. And if it can be safely contained in a solid plastic cube, then wouldn't that automatically downgrade it to no more than Euclid?

Also, like yoric said, the photo and the description don't match. Particularly in regards to the horns.

But still: why? This article basically boils down to:

Class: Used to be Keter
Containment: stick it in a box, waiting to get trashed
Description: it's a carved skull. dunno what it does
Addendum: TRAITOROUS BOX!

So why was it keter? What did/does it do? Why is it safe/neutralized now that it's stuck in the middle of a plastic cube? There's so little information here about why I should be scared of it that I'm more confused than anything else.

Looking back at your criticisms of the original archived copy, the only thing I can figure is that the iconic humorous line still bothers you, unless I'm missing something else? What else feels off?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This still has all the problems that made me dislike the original, but the addition of the kickstarter-ness amused me just enough that I'm no-voting rather than downvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was not under that impression, but I've also been out of the loop for a while. If there's a hold on archive rewrites, I can take this down for the time being.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, the first rewrite draft was in past tense because the original was in past tense. Fixing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked some of this, like the rewritten computer-bit and the ending, but found the main description kinda enh. In the end, though, it wasn't enough to keep me from upvoting.

Side note: I thought TroyL wanted to save the -ARC rewrites until a good number were done. Is that plan just gone, or what?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Considering the spelling, that "jake" was appearantly ripped off. What kind of limited edition is that? Full of errors and no real manual. He just got a beta-version. Satanists these days…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it. It tickles me in diabolical places.

One thing though:

Subjects exposed to SCP-1857 for extended periods of time often exhibited increased aggression and anger, and a small number of subjects, particularly those of [REDACTED] ancestry, were prone to entering a comatose state when exposed to SCP-1857 for periods exceeding fifteen (15) minutes. Furthermore, affected subjects also exhibit increased levels of interest in mythical supernatural entities and the occult that increases with time.

You seem to switch to past tense and then halfway through back to present tense. Was that your intention?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No problem.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, at the time of this post, it's far from the "keeper" range just yet, so we shall see. :) I'm gonna let it sit for a bit, see how it does before I consider any additional changes. Thanks for the feedback, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, I'm not knockin' ya for trying to bring it up to today's standards (and, given the rating, being successful). I'm just noting that this is less enjoyable than the original by a significant margin.

If you wanna keep this in its current form, though, I might suggest reorganizing it so that "TRAITOROUS BOX" is at the bottom. Works better as a punchline.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The problem, though, was that the original had so little to show that all you were left with was a ridiculously cliche object. Given, it's cliche by today's standards and not back then, but the sum result is that the original is archived and would be shot down in a moment's notice if posted today.

I did my best, and I posted it knowing full well that it was ridiculously dorky. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly? This shows waaaay too much. The key strength of the original was not giving very much information, which made the log (which you've at least kept mostly intact) both scarier and funnier.

But now you have some sort of idea what's going on here and it's lame.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Infinitely better than the original.
+1
(Although I'm still pissed about my thetans not carrying over from previous versions)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a rewrite of SCP-257-ARC, posted on 19 Aug 2008 by DrakeRunner.

This also bears some explanation:

The original SCP had been archived for being the first on-site reference of the challenge-phrase, "Does the Black Moon howl?". A long-time in-joke amongst staff, my personal headcanon is that it's the trigger phrase for a small, benign memetic agent that's embedded in all staff assigned to work on mind-affecting SCPs, especially those with an occult bent to them.

When a mentally healthy researcher is given the trigger phrase, they will reflexively answer with the response, "Only when waning." If a compromised researcher is given the trigger phrase, the embedded agent will trigger a momentary breakdown that results in all sorts of absurd responses.

Edit: Other things I need to point out —

  • If it's not clear enough, this is meant to be a parody of RPG Maker. All credit where it's due.
  • It may be overly subtle, but "Limited Founder's Edition" is supposed to imply that this was, well, Kickstarted. As in, "Pledge $1000 today and you will get a Limited Founder's Edition with steel case, art book, and a letter of thanks signed by all of the development team! (25 available)"
